titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Oppé letters to Janet Camp Troxell
titlestmt.author: edited by Francis Lapka

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 1, 2026 at 18:00

did.unittitle: Paul Oppé letters to Janet Camp Troxell

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

did.abstract: The collection comprises letters from Paul Oppé to Janet Camp Troxell, with related material.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is unprocessed and may not be available for immediate access.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The collection is the physical property of the Yale Center for British Art. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the Archives Department.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Oppé letters to Janet Camp Troxell. Yale Center for British Art, Paul Mellon Collection.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Yale Center for British Art, Paul Mellon Collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises letters from Paul Oppé to Janet Camp Troxell, with related material. The bulk of the collection comprises letters written from Oppé to Troxell between 1950 and 1957 (items 1-79). Also present: two Christmas cards from Oppé to Troxell, 1955-1956 (items 80-81); three postcards from Oppé to Troxell, 1953-1955 (items 82-84); one letter from Armide Oppé to Troxell, 1955 (item 85); one letter from William Mills Ivins to Troxell, 1954 (item 86); two letters from Joan Hassall to Troxell, 1953 (items 87-88); and one letter and printed invitation from Marit Guinness Aschan to Troxell, 1955 (items 89-90).

The Janet Camp Troxell letters collection contains a number of letters sent to Janet Camp Troxell from a variety of correspondents from the period of 1950 to 1957. There are single letters from Armide Oppé, William Mills Ivins, Joan Hassall, and Marit Guiness Aschan. The rest of the correspondence is from art historian and collector Paul Oppé. Oppé's letters largely concern prints and drawings he has seen in Europe that would appeal to Camp Troxell. These include Dante Gabriel Rossetti pieces, which align with Camp Troxell's specialty. He also includes prices for some works and appears to be acting as an informal art advisor. The letters are friendly and discuss mutual friends such as Yale University professor Chauncey Brewster Tinker, as well as family friends. Oppé was writing to Camp Troxell until 1957, the year of his death.
